cards.text: SUHAKAM: 500,000 to 1 million stateless children in Sabah — among the largest statelessness concentrations in Southeast Asia.
cards.sub: Tens of thousands are excluded from formal schooling per UNICEF. Official statistics structurally underplay the true scale.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: Federal Constitution Article 14 grants citizenship by birth under specific conditions — stateless children fall through gaps in jus soli and jus sanguinis.
cards.s: The conditions assume documentation that the children's parents often do not have, perpetuating statelessness across generations.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: Malaysia has not ratified the 1961 Convention on the Reduction of Statelessness — leaving no treaty obligation to provide a path to citizenship.
cards.s: SUHAKAM has repeatedly recommended ratification. The recommendation has not been acted upon by successive governments.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: The Bajau Laut (Sea Gypsy) community is the most visible affected group — described by researchers as 'genuinely stateless' across Malaysia, Indonesia and the Philippines.
cards.s: Many Bajau Laut children were born in Sabah and have never set foot in another country, but no state recognises them as citizens.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: The story is not 12,000 invisible children — it is potentially a million, and the official enumeration system cannot see them at all.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: Statelessness in Sabah is structural exclusion — it needs citizenship-pathway legislation and the will to count the population honestly.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

issue_headline: SUHAKAM Estimates Half a Million to One Million Stateless Children in Sabah
